core_vision.summary: Self-evolving math-driven art engine with three interlocking loops
core_vision.loop_1_internal: Local autonomous evolution: math models + knowledge constraints guide generation throughout the entire pipeline (not just final scoring). Must run without AI (AUTONOMOUS mode) and never block on missing AI connection.
core_vision.loop_2_distillation: Continuous Workflow: User uploads PDFs/books/Sprites to Manus chat -> Manus manually distills into knowledge/*.md and math constraints -> Manus optimizes local evolution pipeline based on new knowledge -> pushes to GitHub -> user pulls and runs evolution.
core_vision.loop_3_math_mining: Manus proactively searches academic papers, GitHub projects, Reddit for relevant math models -> converts to code -> registers in the math registry.
quality_principles: No invalid iterations: detect consecutive identical outputs, diagnose root cause, report and halt safely
Knowledge permeates everything: math and art rules guide every stage of generation
Simplicity at core: external tools (GPU, Unity, SD) are optional add-ons
Pseudo-3D ready: preserve extension paths for future pseudo-3D rendering
Cross-session continuity: every new conversation picks up via SESSION_HANDOFF.md
Deduplication without loss: remove duplicates but never discard valuable knowledge
core_vision.collaboration_model: Manus executes code/knowledge tasks -> pushes to GitHub -> User pulls, runs, reports results -> Manus optimizes. User handles local-only tasks (GPU setup, software install).

strict_completion_policy.summary: A task only counts as truly complete when the end-user requirement is satisfied end-to-end without hidden manual glue, placeholder implementations, or behavior that contradicts the core vision.

pending_tasks.description: Align invalid-iteration handling with the documented diagnose-and-safe-halt quality principle
pending_tasks.notes: Strict audit gap from SESSION-014. The core vision says invalid iterations must be diagnosed, reported, and halted safely for review, but ArtMathQualityController.iteration_end currently auto-recovers instead of stopping when autonomous mode hits HUMAN_REQUIRED stagnation. This is a direct behavior-vs-requirement mismatch, not merely an optimization opportunity.

pending_tasks.description: Add a finite-difference gradient optimizer path for CPU-only inner-loop acceleration
pending_tasks.notes: Strict audit reconfirmed in SESSION-014. README and knowledge/differentiable_rendering.md define finite-difference gradients as the next no-GPU optimization path, but the inner loop still relies solely on evolutionary search.

pending_tasks.description: Generalize the evolution run workflow beyond the built-in texture demo target
pending_tasks.notes: Strict audit gap from SESSION-014. mathart-evolve run exists, but the CLI only exposes choices=["texture"] and therefore does not yet represent a true end-user evolution workflow for sprites, animations, or level-bound assets. The current command is a real milestone, but still a narrow built-in demo path rather than the broader local evolution engine promised by the project vision.

pending_tasks.description: Build a reference-image to parameter inference workflow (Image-to-Math bootstrap)
pending_tasks.notes: Strict audit reconfirmed in SESSION-014. The project can ingest sprite references and evaluate against them, but it still lacks a workflow that infers parameter seeds or math-model hints directly from uploaded reference images. Uploading references is therefore not the same as satisfying the long-term Image-to-Math requirement.

pending_tasks.description: Expand MathPaperMiner source coverage to include Reddit or equivalent community discovery channels
pending_tasks.notes: Strict audit gap from SESSION-014. Loop 3 is documented as searching academic papers, GitHub projects, and Reddit discussions, but the live implementation currently covers arXiv and GitHub only. Real-source mining therefore remains only partially aligned with the stated discovery scope.

pending_tasks.description: Expose the level-aware export bridge through a true end-to-end CLI or workspace workflow
pending_tasks.notes: Strict audit gap from SESSION-014. The LevelSpecBridge-to-ExportBridge connection is implemented at the API layer, but mathart-export still only supports --generate-demo or manual scripting. A real user-facing export workflow that consumes level-aware specs without custom glue code is still missing.

pending_tasks.description: Promote mined scaffolds into concrete math implementations and stable registry entries
pending_tasks.notes: Strict audit reconfirmed in SESSION-014. The third loop now closes through registry-backed scaffolds, but promoted modules are still executable placeholders. The project still needs a workflow that turns high-value mined candidates into real mathematical implementations, evaluator hooks, and eventual stable registry graduation.
Code skeletons for differentiable rendering, Unity Shader knowledge, and Pseudo-3D are already implemented. Deep integration requires user to provide NVIDIA GPU (CUDA 11+) and/or Unity Editor access.

Completed in SESSION-009. InnerLoopRunner now detects generators that expose progress_callback, invokes the mid_generation checkpoint during multi-step generation, and honors retry decisions. However, strict audit in SESSION-014 identified a follow-up requirement gap: autonomous stagnation handling still does not fully match the documented diagnose-and-safe-halt principle, which is now tracked separately as TASK-016.

Completed in SESSION-006. The CLI can ingest sprite references and spritesheets. Strict audit in SESSION-014 keeps Image-to-Math inference open as TASK-014 because upload alone does not infer parameter seeds or model hints.

Completed in SESSION-012. AssetExporter now accepts LevelSpecBridge-derived AssetSpec inputs, validates sprite dimensions, frame counts, palette limits, and tile sizing before export, and writes level-aware metadata into export manifests. Strict audit in SESSION-014 keeps the end-user export workflow open as TASK-018 because the CLI still exposes only demo generation or manual scripting.

Completed in SESSION-007. Workspace inbox, file picker, and output classification exist. Strict audit in SESSION-014 keeps end-to-end level-aware export workflow open as TASK-018 because workspace plumbing is not yet connected to a real export command path.

pending_tasks.description: Add CLI Command to Run Evolution Loop
pending_tasks.notes: Completed in SESSION-009. Added mathart-evolve run with a built-in texture evolution target, progress-aware generation, output image saving, and JSON run metadata. Strict audit in SESSION-014 keeps generalized real-asset execution open as TASK-019 because the CLI currently exposes only the built-in texture target.

Completed in SESSION-010. Upgraded EvolutionaryOptimizer with adaptive mutation schedules, diversity tracking, elite-guided local search, random immigrants, and a lightweight cma_es_like strategy for higher-dimensional search without external dependencies. Strict audit in SESSION-014 keeps finite-difference CPU acceleration open as TASK-013 because optimizer upgrades did not yet add the promised gradient-approximation path.

Completed in SESSION-011. MathPaperMiner now queries the real arXiv Atom API and GitHub Search REST API, derives structured relevance scores from public metadata, supports optional GitHub token auth via environment variables, keeps the LLM as a fallback path, and adds regression coverage for live-source parsing and orchestration. Strict audit in SESSION-014 keeps broader Loop-3 source coverage open as TASK-017 because Reddit or equivalent community mining is still absent.

pending_tasks.description: Close the math mining loop from discovery candidates to registry-backed implementation scaffolds
pending_tasks.notes: Completed in SESSION-013. MathPaperMiner now enriches mined candidates with registry metadata, can scaffold executable Python modules plus smoke-test files, persists promoted entries into math_models.json-compatible registry records, stores registry candidate manifests, and exposes a CLI --promote path to close the loop from accepted mined papers to runnable scaffolds. Strict audit in SESSION-014 keeps scaffold-to-real-model graduation open as TASK-015 because promoted modules are still placeholders rather than concrete mathematical implementations.

capability_gaps.name: DIFFERENTIABLE_RENDERING
capability_gaps.description: Differentiable rendering requires NVIDIA GPU for real-time parameter gradients

capability_gaps.name: UNITY_SHADER_PREVIEW
capability_gaps.description: Unity Shader preview requires Unity Editor for live rendering feedback

capability_gaps.name: AI_IMAGE_MODEL
capability_gaps.description: High-quality sprite generation requires a diffusion model (e.g., SDXL-Turbo)
capability_gaps.requires: GPU + Stable Diffusion API or local model

strict_gap_audit.summary: Revalidated the project against the strict completion standard at end of day. No new end-user-visible closures were found beyond the backlog already identified in SESSION-014, so the pending-task list remains the canonical map of unsatisfied product requirements rather than a mix of finished features and near-complete milestones.
key_findings: Autonomous stagnation handling still contradicts the documented safe-halt requirement when HUMAN_REQUIRED is reached.
The run CLI is still limited to a built-in texture target and does not yet represent the broader local evolution workflow promised by the project vision.
Level-aware export is connected at the API layer but not yet exposed as a true end-to-end CLI or workspace workflow.
Live math mining currently covers arXiv and GitHub only, leaving the documented Reddit/community discovery scope unimplemented.
Promoted mined candidates land in executable scaffolds, not in concrete mathematical implementations.
Today’s closing audit did not identify additional hidden completions; the existing strict backlog remains valid and prioritized for future sessions.

evolution_history.notes: Major architecture upgrade: self-evolving brain system fully operational
TASK-002 complete, TASK-001 bug fix, CLI sprite workflow operational
TEXTURE gap closed, workspace management operational, 10 math models
Audit & task integration: comprehensive requirement review, task deduplication, expanded scopes, added TASK-008, recorded core vision with three-loop architecture
Completed TASK-001 and TASK-009: added mathart-evolve run, integrated mid_generation checkpoint into InnerLoopRunner, added scipy-free QC fallback, and extended regression coverage.

Completed TASK-010: upgraded EvolutionaryOptimizer with adaptive GA controls, elite-guided local search, random immigrants, cma_es_like sampling, and expanded regression coverage verified across distill, evolution, and quality test suites.

Completed TASK-011: connected MathPaperMiner to the real arXiv Atom API and GitHub Search REST API, added optional authenticated GitHub requests, retained LLM fallback behavior, and verified live-source parsing plus project-memory compatibility through regression tests.

Completed TASK-003: connected LevelSpecBridge to AssetExporter with level-aware export validation and metadata. Also performed a comprehensive gap audit, which promoted mine-to-registry automation to the top pending task and added finite-difference plus Image-to-Math roadmap tasks.

Completed TASK-012: closed the math-mining loop to registry-backed scaffolds by enriching registry candidates, adding module and smoke-test scaffolding, saving promoted entries into math_models.json-compatible records, exposing a CLI promote path, and refreshing the product-gap audit to highlight finite-difference, Image-to-Math, and scaffold-to-real-model follow-up work.

Performed a strict completion audit: only true end-to-end requirement fulfillment now counts as complete. Reframed previously over-broad interpretations into explicit follow-up tasks, added TASK-016 through TASK-019, and refreshed project state so future sessions can attack the remaining gaps one by one without mistaking milestones for finished product outcomes.

Performed an end-of-day strict audit refresh. Revalidated the SESSION-014 backlog against the codebase and user-visible workflows, confirmed that no additional features should be promoted to 'truly complete', and kept TASK-016, TASK-013, TASK-019, TASK-014, TASK-017, TASK-018, TASK-015, and TASK-005 as the authoritative attack order for future work.
